methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Xaira Therapeutics is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Marc Tessier-Lavigne that has raised $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a narrow comp universe of 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private comparables including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics, though none provide sufficient overlap in stage and valuation tier for robust peer-median anchoring.

Given the limited applicability of peer comparables at seed stage, the primary valuation method employed is stage-progression analysis. Using the $1.0B total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $2.5B. This forms the median point of the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.1B to $2.9B, with $2.5B as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue data—precluding application of the 6.6x public sector P/S median—and the lack of sufficiently granular private comparables at identical stage and valuation tier. The single-method foundation of this estimate reflects data constraints rather than conviction, and any external financing or partnership developments would warrant recalibration.
